\label{t:odd}
Let $p$ be odd and $\Sigma, T$, and $\Omega$ as in Definition \ref{def:am_modp}. If $\dim (\Sigma)=\dim (T)+1 = m+1$, then $\mathrm{Sing} (T)$ is locally a classical free boundary outside of a relatively closed $\mathcal{S}$ which is countably $(m-2)$-rectifiable and has locally finite $\mathcal{H}^{m-2}$ measure.

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{t:even}
Let $p\geq 3$ be arbitrary (i.e. odd or even) and $\Sigma, T$, and $\Omega$ as in Definition \ref{def:am_modp}. If $\dim (\Sigma)=m+1$ then $\mathrm{Sing} (T)$ is a classical free boundary in a neighborhood of $q\in \mathrm{Sing} (T)$ if and only if one tangent cone $\mathbf{C}$ to $T$ at $q$ is not flat \footnote{We say that a tangent cone $\mathbf{C}$ to $T$ at $q$ is \emph{flat} if $\mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C})$ is contained in an $m$-dimensional linear subspace of $T_q\Sigma$. Moreover it is well known that the directions in which the cone is translation-invariant form a vector space, commonly called the spine of $\mathbf{C}$.} and it is invariant with respect to translations along $(m-1)$ linearly independent directions.
semantic_equivalence_artifact.normalized_natural_language_theorem: Quantifiers: arbitrary; one Assumptions: p ≥ 3; dim(Σ) = m + 1 Conclusion: Sing(T) is a classical free boundary in a neighborhood of q ∈ Sing(T) if and only if one tangent cone C to T at q is not flat and it is invariant with respect to translations along (m-1) linearly independent directions

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{theorem}\label{t:p=3} Let $p=3$, and let $\Sigma, \Omega$, and $T$ be as in Definition \ref{def:am_modp}. If $\dim (\Sigma)= \dim (T)+1=m+1$, then $\mathcal{S}$ is empty for $m\leq 2$, and it is $(m-3)$-rectifiable with locally finite $\mathcal{H}^{m-3}$ measure for $m\geq 3$. If $\dim (\Sigma)\geq \dim (T) + 2=m+2$, then $\mathcal{S}$ is $(m-2)$-rectifiable and has locally finite $\mathcal{H}^{m-2}$ measure. \end{theorem}

\label{p:example}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{theorem}[Uniqueness of cylindrical blow-ups]\label{t:main} Let $p\in \mathbb N\setminus \{0,1,2\}$ and $\mathbf{C}_0$ be as in Assumption \ref{ass:cone}. There are constants $\bar \eta>0$ and $C$ depending only on $p,m,n$, and $\mathbf{C}_0$ with the following property. If $T, \Sigma, \Omega$ and $q$ are as in Assumption \ref{ass:cone} with $\eta=\bar \eta$, then the tangent cone $\mathbf{C}$ to $T$ at $q$ is unique, has $(m-1)$-dimensional spine, and moreover the following decay estimates hold for every radius $r\leq 1$ \begin{equation}\label{e:decay-always} \frac{1}{r^{m+2}}\int_{\mathbf{B}_r (q)}\mathrm{dist}^2(\bar q - q, \mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C})) \, d\|T\| (\bar q) \leq C\, (\mathbf{E}_0 + \mathbf{A}^{1/2}) r^{\frac{1}{2}} \, , \end{equation} \begin{equation}\label{e:decay-flat} \hat\mathscr{F}^p_{\mathbf{B}_1} ((\eta_{q,r})_\sharp T - \mathbf{C}) \leq C (\mathbf{E}_0^{1/2}+\mathbf{A}^{1/4}) r^\frac{1}{4}\, . \end{equation} In particular $\hat\mathscr{F}^p_{\mathbf{B}_1} (\mathbf{C}-\mathbf{C}_0) \leq C (\mathbf{E}_0^{1/2}+\mathbf{A}^{1/4}) + \bar\eta$. \end{theorem}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{proposition} \label{lem:structure_cones} Let $p \geq 2$ be an integer, and let $\mathbf{C}$ be an $m$-dimensional area minimizing cone ${\rm mod}(p)$ in $\mathbb{R}^{m+n}$ with spine $V$ of dimension $m-1$. Let $V^\perp$ be the orthogonal complement of $V$. Then: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] $\mathbf{C} = \mathbf{C}' \times \left\llbracket{V}\right\rrbracket $ for some $1$-dimensional area minimizing cone ${\rm mod}(p)$ in $V^\perp$ (where we assume to have fixed a choice of a constant orientation $\vec{\tau}$ on $V$); \item[(ii)] There exist $N\geq 3$ distinct vectors $v(1), \ldots, v(N)\in \mathbb{S}^n \subset V^\perp$ and $N$ positive integers $\kappa(1), \ldots, \kappa(N) \in \left[1,\sfrac{p}{2}\right) \cap \mathbb{Z}$ such that \begin{itemize} \item[•] if $\ell(i) := \{t \,v(i) : t\geq 0\} \subset V^\perp$ is oriented in such a way that $\partial \left\llbracket{\ell(i)}\right\rrbracket = - \left\llbracket{0}\right\rrbracket$, then either $\mathbf{C}' = \sum_{i=1}^N \kappa(i) \left\llbracket{\ell(i)}\right\rrbracket $ or $\mathbf{C}' = - \sum_{i=1}^N \kappa(i) \left\llbracket{\ell(i)}\right\rrbracket$; \item[•] $\sum_{i=1}^N \kappa(i) v(i) = 0$. \end{itemize} \item[(iii)] $\sum_{i=1}^N \kappa(i) = p$, and hence the $m$-dimensional density of $\mathbf{C}$ at $0$ is $\Theta_{\mathbf{C}}(0) = \frac{p}{2}$. \end{itemize} Moreover, when $ p$ is odd, the set of area minimizing cones ${\rm mod}(p)$ with spine of dimension $m-1$ is compact with respect to the flat topology. \end{proposition}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{theorem}[Decay estimate]\label{t:decay} Let $p \geq 2$ be an integer and let $\mathbf{C}_0$ be as in Assumption \ref{ass:cone} with $q=0$. There are constants $C$, $\eta>0$, and $\rho > 0$, depending only on $m,n,p$, and $\mathbf{C}_0$ with the following property. Assume that $\Sigma$ and $T$ are as in the Assumption \ref{assumption:manifolds and currents} with $R_0=1$, that $\hat\mathscr{F}^p_{\mathbf{B}_1}(T-\mathbf{C}_0) \leq \eta$ and that $\mathbf{C}$ is a representative ${\rm mod}(p)$ cycle (not necessarily area-minimizing), with the following properties: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] $\mathbf{C}$ is a cone coherent with $\mathbf{C}_0$ and $\vartheta (\mathbf{C}, \mathbf{C}_0) \leq \eta$; \item[(ii)] $\max\{ \mathbf{E} (T,\mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}), 0, 1), \mathbf{A}^{\sfrac 12}\}\leq \eta$. \end{itemize} Then there is a representative ${\rm mod}(p)$ cycle $\mathbf{C}'$ which is a cone coherent with $\mathbf{C}_0$ and satisfies the following estimates: \begin{align} &\max \{\mathbf{E}(T, \mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}'), 0, \rho), (\rho \mathbf{A})^{\sfrac 12}\} \leq \rho^{1/2} \max\{\mathbf{E}(T,\mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}), 0, 1),\mathbf{A}^{\sfrac 12}\}\, , \label{e:decay}\\ &\hat\mathscr{F}^p_{\mathbf{B}_1} ((\eta_{0, \rho})_\sharp T-\mathbf{C}') \leq C (\mathbf{E}(T,\mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}), 0, 1) + \mathbf{A})^{\sfrac 12}\, ,\label{e:flat-C'}\\ &\vartheta (\mathbf{C}', \mathbf{C}_0) \leq \vartheta (\mathbf{C}, \mathbf{C}_0) + C (\mathbf{E}(T,\mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}), 0, 1) + \mathbf{A})^{\sfrac 12}\, .\label{e:incremento-angolo} \end{align} \end{theorem}

Then there is $\eta_1 = \eta_1 (\mathbf{S})>0$ with the following property. If $T_j \in \mathscr{R}_m(\mathbf{B}_2)$ are such that \begin{equation} \label{compactness assumption} \sup_j \|T_j\|(\mathbf{B}_{\sfrac{3}{2}}) < \infty\,, \qquad (\partialT_j) \mathbin{\vrule height 1.6ex depth 0pt width %measure restriction 0.13ex\vrule height 0.13ex depth 0pt width 1.3ex}\mathbf{B}_{\sfrac{3}{2}} = 0 \; {\rm mod}(p)\,, \end{equation} and \begin{equation} \label{small flat assumption} \hat\mathscr{F}^p_{\mathbf{B}_1} (T_j - \mathbf{C}) < \eta_1 \qquad \forall j\, , \end{equation} then \begin{equation} \label{qualitative flat_excess} \lim_{j\to\infty} \mathbf{E}(T_j, \mathbf{S},0, 1) = 0 \quad \implies \quad \lim_{j\to \infty} \hat\mathscr{F}^p_{\mathbf{B}_1}(T_j - \mathbf{C}) = 0\,. \end{equation} \end{lemma}

If $T$ and $\mathbf{C}$ are as in Assumptions \ref{a:graphical} and \ref{a:graphical2}, $\mathbf{C}$ satisfies \eqref{e:hyp_graph_cone} for some $\varepsilon_1 < \bar{\varepsilon}_1$, and \begin{equation} \label{e:ipotesi di cubo} Q \in \mathcal{W}(T, \mathbf{S}, \tau) \qquad \mbox{for some $\tau < \bar\tau$}\,, \end{equation} then: \begin{itemize} \item[(a)] $\mathrm{spt} (T)\cap \{(x,y): (|x|,y)\in Q\} \subset {\rm P}_0^{-1} (\mathcal{C} (4Q, \delta))$; \item[(b)] there exists a $p$-multifunction $u^Q = \{u^Q_{i,j}\} \in C^{1,\frac12}(4Q)$ over $\mathbf{S}_0$ with $u^Q_{i,j} \colon 4 Q_i \subset \mathbf{H}_{0,i} \to \mathbf{H}_{0,i}^{\perp_0}$ for all $i$ and $j$ and \begin{equation} \label{e:Holder estimate on functions} \| u^Q \|_{C^{1,\frac12}(4Q)} \leq C_1 \, \delta \end{equation} for some constant $C_1 = C_1(m,p) > 0$, and such that \begin{equation} \label{T' is graphical} T' \mathbin{\vrule height 1.6ex depth 0pt width %measure restriction 0.13ex\vrule height 0.13ex depth 0pt width 1.3ex}\mathcal{C}(4Q,\delta) = \mathbf{G}_{\mathbf{S}_0}(u^Q) \,. \end{equation} \end{itemize} \end{lemma}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{corollary}\label{c:ovvio} Let $T$, $\Sigma$, $\mathbf{C}$, $\mathbf{C}_0$, $\mathbf{S} := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C})$, and $\mathbf{S}_0 := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}_0)$ be as in Theorem \ref{thm:graph_v1}, and consider the corresponding map $v$ and $U_{4\mathcal{W}}$ its domain. Then: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] there is a linear $p$-multifunction $l$ over $\mathbf{S}_0$ such that $\mathbf{C} = \mathbf{G}_{\mathbf{S}_0} (l)$; \item[(ii)] ${\rm dist}\, (q, \mathbf{S}) = {\rm \mathrm{dist}}\, \left(q, \cup_h\, \mathrm{spt} \left(\mathbf{G}_{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}} (l_{i,h}) \right)\right)$ for each $q\in \mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{G}_{\mathbf{S}_0} (v_{i,j}) )$; \item[(iii)] there is a geometric constant $C$ such that \begin{equation}\label{e:L2-graphical-initial} C^{-1} \int_{R_\mathcal{W}} \mathrm{dist}(q, \mathbf{S})^2 \, d\|T\| (q) \leq \sum_{Q \in \mathcal W} \sum_{i=1}^{N_0} \int_{4 Q_i} \sum_{j=1}^{\kappa_{0,i}} \min_{1 \leq h \leq \kappa_{0,i}} |v_{i,j} (z) - l_{i,h} (z)|^2\, dz \leq C \, \mathbf{E}\,. \end{equation} \end{itemize} \end{corollary}

Conclusion: \mathbf{S} := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}); \mathbf{S}_0 := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}_0); \{\tilde l_{i,j} = l_{i,h(i,j)}\}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{theorem}[Improved estimate]\label{thm:graph v2} Let $T$, $\Sigma$, $\mathbf{C}$, $\mathbf{C}_0$, $\mathbf{S} := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C})$, and $\mathbf{S}_0 := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}_0)$ be as in Theorem \ref{thm:graph_v1}, let $u$ and $U_{4 \mathcal{W}}$ be the corresponding map and its domain, and let $l$ be as in Corollary \ref{c:ovvio}(i). There are a geometric constant $C$ and a selection function $h:(i,j)\mapsto h (i,j) \in \{1, \ldots \kappa_{0,i}\}$ such that if $\tilde l$ denotes the linear $p$-multifunction $\{\tilde l_{i,j} = l_{i,h(i,j)}\}$ and $w$ denotes the $p$-multifunction on $U_{4\mathcal W}$ over $\mathbf{S}_0$ defined by \begin{equation} \label{e:difference function} w_{i,j} := u_{i,j} - \tilde l_{i,j}\,, \end{equation} then \begin{align}\label{eqn:Linfty estimate excess} \sup_{\zeta = (t,y) \in U_{3 \mathcal W}}t^{\frac{m}{2}+1}\left( t^{-1} \lvert#1\rvert{w(\zeta)} + \lvert#1\rvert{Dw(\zeta)}+ t^{\sfrac12} [Dw]_{\sfrac12}(\zeta) \right)&\le C\, (\mathbf{E}+\mathbf{A}^2)^{\sfrac12}\,,\\ \label{L2 estimate excess} \sum_{Q \in \mathcal W} \sum_{i=1}^{N_0} \sum_{j=1}^{\kappa_{0,i}} \int_{3 Q_i} (|w_{i,j} (z)|^2 + \lvert#1\rvert{x}^2 \lvert#1\rvert{Dw_{i,j}(z)}^2) \, dz &\le C\, (\mathbf{E}+\mathbf{A}^2) \,, \end{align} where, for $z \in 3 Q_i$, $|x|$ denotes, as usual, the distance of $z$ from $V$. \end{theorem}

theorem cor_reparametrization
 (T C C0 S S0 S_tilde u_tilde v_tilde w E A r η_tilde eps1 η_S0 : ℝ)
 (h_S : S = spt C)
 (h_S0 : S0 = spt C0)
 (h_S_tilde : S_tilde = open_book S0)
 (h_u_tilde : u_tilde = p_multifunction S_tilde)
 (h_v_tilde : v_tilde = p_multifunction S_tilde)
 (h_w : w = p_multifunction S0)
 (h_r : 0 < r ∧ r < 2)
 (h_η_tilde : η_tilde > 0)
 (h_eps1 : eps1 > 0)
 (h_η_S0 : η_S0 > 0) :
 ∃ u_tilde : ℝ,
 (∀ i j, u_tilde_{i,j} : (U_{2 * W})_{i,j} → H_{i,j}^⊥) ∧
 ‖u_tilde‖_{C^{1,1/2}(U_{2 * W})} ≤ beta ∧
 (T ⊓ R_W = G_{S_tilde}(v_tilde) ⊓ R_W) ∧
 (∫_{(U_{2 * W})_{i,j} ∩ B2} (|u_tilde_{i,j}|^2 + |x|^2 * |grad u_tilde_{i,j}|^2) ≤
 C * ∫_{(U_{3 * W})_i ∩ B3} (|w_{i,j}|^2 + |x|^2 * |grad w_{i,j}|^2) ∧
 C * (E + A^2)) ∧
 (r^{m + 2} * E T S_tilde, 0, r ≤ C * (E + A^2)) ∧
 (F_p^{B_{R0}}(C_tilde - C) < η_tilde)

semantic_equivalence_artifact.normalized_natural_language_theorem: Quantifiers: forall; exists Assumptions: \label{cor:reparametrization} Let $T, \Sigma, \mathbf{C}, \mathbf{C}_0, \mathbf{S} := \mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C})$, and $\mathbf{S}_0:= \mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}_0)$ be as in Theorem \ref{thm:graph v2}, and let $\tilde{\mathbf{S}}$ be the open book in Definition \ref{d:new-cone}.; There exists a $p$-multifunction $\tilde u = \{\tilde u_{i,j}\}$ over $\tilde{\mathbf{S}}$ of class $C^{1,\frac12}$ on $U_{2 \mathcal W}$ and with $\tilde u_{i,j} \colon (\tilde U_{2 \mathcal W})_{i,j} \subset \tilde{\mathbf{H}}_{i,j} \to \tilde{\mathbf{H}}_{i,j}^{\perp_0}$ for all $i$ and $j$ such that \begin{equation} \label{e:rep_Holder} \| \tilde u \|_{C^{1,\frac12}(U_{2\mathcal W})} \leq \beta\,, \end{equation} and, denoting $\tilde v$ the $p$-multifunction on $U_{2 \mathcal W}$ over $\tilde \mathbf{S}$ defined by \begin{equation} \label{e:rep_up to manifold} \tilde v_{i,j}(z) := \tilde u_{i,j}(z) + \Psi(z + \tilde u_{i,j}(z))\,, \end{equation} we have \begin{equation}\label{e:rep_parametrization} T \mathbin{\vrule height 1.6ex depth 0pt width %measure restriction 0.13ex\vrule height 0.13ex depth 0pt width 1.3ex}R_{\mathcal W} = {\bf G}_{\tilde{\mathbf{S}}}(\tilde v) \mathbin{\vrule height 1.6ex depth 0pt width %measure restriction 0.13ex\vrule height 0.13ex depth 0pt width 1.3ex}R_{\mathcal W} \end{equation} and \begin{equation}\label{e:comparison-tilde-u-w} \int_{(\tilde U_{2\mathcal W})_{i,j} \cap \mathbf{B}_2} (|\tilde{u}_{i,j}|^2 + |x|^2 |\nabla \tilde{u}_{i,j}|^2) \leq C \int_{(U_{3 \mathcal W})_i\cap \mathbf{B}_3} (|w_{i,j}|^2 + |x|^2 |\nabla w_{i,j}|^2) \leq C \, (\mathbf{E}+ \mathbf{A}^2)\,, \end{equation} where $w$ is the $p$-multifunction over $\mathbf{S}_0$ defined in \eqref{e:difference function}.; \end{equation} Finally, for every fixed $\tilde{\eta}>0$, if $\varepsilon_1$ in \eqref{e:hyp_graph_cone}-\eqref{e:hyp_graph_current} and $\eta_{\mathbf{S}_0}$ in \eqref{e:hyp_flat_T_C0} are chosen sufficiently small, then \begin{equation}\label{e:flat_C_tildeC} \hat\mathscr{F}^p_{\mathbf{B}_{R_0}} (\tilde{\mathbf{C}} - \mathbf{C}) < \tilde{\eta}\, . Conclusion: Moreover, combining this with \eqref{e:L^2 estimate} we trivially have \begin{equation}\label{eq:tildeexcesvsexcess} r^{m+2}\mathbf{E}(T,\tilde\mathbf{S}, 0, r)\leq C\,(\mathbf{E}+\mathbf{A}^2)\,,\qquad 0<r<2\,. \end{equation}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{lemma}[Local linear selection]\label{l:local_selection} There is a constant $C$ depending only on $m$ and $p$ such that the following holds. Let $T$, $\Sigma$, $\mathbf{C}$, $\mathbf{C}_0$, $\mathbf{S} := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C})$, and $\mathbf{S}_0 := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}_0)$ be as in Theorem \ref{thm:graph_v1}, let $u$, $v$ and $U_{4 \mathcal{W}} = \cup_{Q \in \mathcal W} 4Q$ be the corresponding maps and their domain, and let $l$ be as in Corollary \ref{c:ovvio}(i). Consider any $i$ and $j$ and any cube $Q\in \mathcal{W}$. Then there is a $\bar h$, which depends on $i$, $j$, and $Q$, such that \begin{align} &d_Q^m \| u_{i,j} - l_{i,\bar h}\|^2_{L^\infty (3 Q_i)} + d_Q^{2+m} \, \|D (u_{i,j} -l_{i,\bar h})\|^2_{L^\infty (3 Q_i)} + d_Q^{3+m} \, [D u_{i,j}]^2_{\frac{1}{2}, 3 Q_i} \nonumber\\ \leq & C \int_{4Q_i} \min_h |u_{i,j} (z) - l_{i,h} (z)|^2\, dz + C \mathbf{A}^2 d_Q^{2+m}\, \label{e:local-selection} \end{align} (where we recall that $d_Q$ denotes the diameter of $Q$). \end{lemma}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{lemma}[Global linear selection]\label{l:global_selection} There is a constant $C$ depending only on $m$ and $p$ such that the following holds. Let $T$, $\Sigma$, $\mathbf{C}$, $\mathbf{C}_0$, $\mathbf{S} := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C})$, and $\mathbf{S}_0 := \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}_0)$ be as in Theorem \ref{thm:graph_v1}, let $u$, $v$ and $U_{4 \mathcal{W}} = \cup_{Q \in \mathcal W} 4Q$ be the corresponding maps and their domain, and let $l$ be as in Corollary \ref{c:ovvio}(i). Consider any $i$ and $j$, let $\hat Q\in \mathcal{W}$ be any cube which does not have any element above (cf. the partial order relation of Definition \ref{def:whitney}) and let $\hat h$ be the index $\bar h$ of Lemma \ref{l:local_selection} corresponding to $i,j$, and $\hat Q$. Then the following two estimates hold \begin{align} & \sup_{Q\in \mathcal{W}} \left(d_Q^m \| u_{i,j} - l_{i,\hat h}\|^2_{L^\infty (3 Q_i)} + d_Q^{2+m} \, \|D (u_{i,j} -l_{i,\hat h})\|^2_{L^\infty (3 Q_i)} + d_Q^{3+m} \, [D u_{i,j}]^2_{\frac{1}{2}, 3 Q_i} \right)\nonumber\\ \leq & C \sum_{Q\in \mathcal{W}} \int_{4Q_i} \min_h |u_{i,j} (z) - l_{i,h} (z)|^2\, dz + C \mathbf{A}^2\label{e:global_selection_1} \end{align} \begin{align} & \sum_{Q\in \mathcal{W}} d_Q^m \left( \| u_{i,j} - l_{i,\hat h}\|^2_{L^\infty (3 Q_i)} + d_Q^2 \, \|D (u_{i,j} -l_{i,\hat h})\|^2_{L^\infty (3 Q_i)} \right)\nonumber\\ \leq & C \sum_{Q\in \mathcal{W}} \int_{4Q_i} \min_h |u_{i,j} (z) - l_{i,h} (z)|^2\, dz + C \mathbf{A}^2\, .\label{e:global_selection_2} \end{align} \end{lemma}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{theorem}\label{t:Hardt-Simon-main} There exists a constant $\beta_1 > 0$ depending only on $\mathbf{S}_0$ with the following property. Let $T,\Sigma,\mathbf{C},\mathbf{C}_0,\mathbf{S}$, and $\mathbf{S}_0$ be as in Assumptions \ref{a:graphical} and \ref{a:graphical2}. For any $\beta \in \left( 0, \beta_1 \right)$ there are constants $C$, $\eta_3$, and $0 <\varepsilon_2 \leq \varepsilon_1$ (where $\varepsilon_1$ was given in Theorem \ref{thm:graph_v1}) depending upon $(m,n,p,\mathbf{S}_0,\beta)$ such that the following conclusion holds. Assume that: \begin{itemize} \item[(a)] \eqref{e:hyp_flat_T_C0}-\eqref{e:the flat smallness parameter}-\eqref{e:hyp_graph_cone}-\eqref{e:hyp_graph_current} are satisfied with $\varepsilon_2$ and $\eta_3$ in place of $\varepsilon_1$ and $\eta_{\mathbf{S}_0}$; \item[(b)] $\{\tilde{l}\} = \{l_{i, h(i,j)}\}$ is the linear $p$-multifunction of Theorem \ref{thm:graph v2} and $\tilde{\mathbf{S}}$ denotes the open book induced by it as in Definition \ref{d:new-cone}; \item[(c)] $q_0=(x_0,y_0)\in (V^\perp \times V)\cap \mathbf{B}_{\sfrac{3}{4}}$ is a point with $\Theta_T (q_0) \geq \Theta_{\mathbf{C}} (0) = \frac{p}{2}$. \end{itemize} Then \begin{equation} \label{e:Hardt-Simon} |x_0|^2 + \int_{\mathbf{B}_1} \frac{{\rm dist}^2\, (q-q_0, \tilde{\mathbf{S}})}{|q-q_0|^{m+\frac74}}\, d\|T\| (q) \leq C (\mathbf{E}+ \mathbf{A})\, . \end{equation} \end{theorem}

extracted_assumptions: \label{l:monot-Jonas} Let $T$ and $\mathbf{C}$ be as in Theorem \ref{t:Hardt-Simon-main}, and assume that $g (q) = |q|^k \,\hat g (\frac{q}{|q|})$ for some $k\geq 1$ and some Lipschitz nonnegative function $\hat g$ on the unit sphere.

Then, for every $2>\alpha>0$ and $R_1 \leq R_0$ we have \begin{align}\label{e.h_k monotonicity} \frac{\alpha}{2} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} \frac{g^2 (q)}{\lvertq\rvert^{m + 2k -\alpha}}\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q) \leq & \frac{m+2k}{R_1^{m+2k-\alpha}} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} g^2 \,d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} + \frac{2}{\alpha} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} \frac{\lvert\nabla g (q)\rvert^2\lvertq^\perp\rvert^2}{\lvertq\rvert^{m+2k-\alpha}}\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q)\nonumber\\ &+ C \mathbf{A}\|\hat g\|_\infty^2 \frac{\left\lVertT\right\rVert(\mathbf{B}_{R_1})}{R_1^{m-\alpha}}\,, \end{align} where $q^\perp := q - \mathbf{p}_{\vec{T}}(q)$ at $\mathcal{H}^m$-a.e.

Moreover, for every nonnegative $f\in C^1 (\mathbb R)$, upon setting $F (t) := -\int_t^{R_1} f'(s) s^m\, ds$, we have \begin{align}\label{e.classical monotonicity with f} & \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} f(|q|)\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{\mathbf{C}} (q) - \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} f(\lvert#1\rvert{q})\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q) + \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} \frac{F(\lvertq\rvert)\,\lvertq^\perp\rvert^2}{\lvertq\rvert^{m+2}}\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q)\nonumber\\ \leq & -\frac{1}{m } \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} \frac{F(\lvertq\rvert)\, q^\perp\cdot H_T (q)}{\lvertq\rvert^{m}}\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q)\,.

abla g (q)vert^2\lvertq^\perpvert^2}{\lvertqvert^{m+2k-\alpha}}\, d\left\lVert#1ightVert{T} (q)
onumber\ &+ C \mathbf{A}\|\hat g\|_\infty^2  rac{\left\lVertTightVert(\mathbf{B}_{R_1})}{R_1^{m-\alpha}}\,, \end{align} where $q^\perp := q - \mathbf{p}_{\vec{T}}(q)$ at $\mathcal{H}^m$-a.e. $q \in \mathrm{spt}(T)$ (here, $\mathbf{p}_{\vec{T}} = \mathbf{p}_{\vec{T}(q)}$ is the orthogonal projection onto ${m span}(\vec{T}(q))$). assumptions: \label{l:monot-Jonas} Let $T$ and $\mathbf{C}$ be as in Theorem \ref{t:Hardt-Simon-main}, and assume that $g (q) = |q|^k \,\hat g (\frac{q}{|q|})$ for some $k\geq 1$ and some Lipschitz nonnegative function $\hat g$ on the unit sphere.

Then, for every $2>\alpha>0$ and $R_1 \leq R_0$ we have \begin{align}\label{e.h_k monotonicity} \frac{\alpha}{2} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} \frac{g^2 (q)}{\lvertq\rvert^{m + 2k -\alpha}}\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q) \leq & \frac{m+2k}{R_1^{m+2k-\alpha}} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} g^2 \,d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} + \frac{2}{\alpha} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} \frac{\lvert\nabla g (q)\rvert^2\lvertq^\perp\rvert^2}{\lvertq\rvert^{m+2k-\alpha}}\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q)\nonumber\\ &+ C \mathbf{A}\|\hat g\|_\infty^2 \frac{\left\lVertT\right\rVert(\mathbf{B}_{R_1})}{R_1^{m-\alpha}}\,, \end{align} where $q^\perp := q - \mathbf{p}_{\vec{T}}(q)$ at $\mathcal{H}^m$-a.e.

Moreover, for every nonnegative $f\in C^1 (\mathbb R)$, upon setting $F (t) := -\int_t^{R_1} f'(s) s^m\, ds$, we have \begin{align}\label{e.classical monotonicity with f} & \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} f(|q|)\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{\mathbf{C}} (q) - \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} f(\lvert#1\rvert{q})\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q) + \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} \frac{F(\lvertq\rvert)\,\lvertq^\perp\rvert^2}{\lvertq\rvert^{m+2}}\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q)\nonumber\\ \leq & -\frac{1}{m } \int_{\mathbf{B}_{R_1}} \frac{F(\lvertq\rvert)\, q^\perp\cdot H_T (q)}{\lvertq\rvert^{m}}\, d\left\lVert#1\right\rVert{T} (q)\,.
translation_statement_schema.claim: $q \in \mathrm{spt}(T)$ (here, $\mathbf{p}_{\vec{T}} = \mathbf{p}_{\vec{T}(q)}$ is the orthogonal projection onto ${\rm span}(\vec{T}(q))$). \end{align}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{proposition}\label{prop:density-est} Under the same assumptions of Proposition \ref{prop:decay-growth-est} (up to possibly choosing a smaller value for $\beta_1$) the following estimate holds. Denote by $\mathbf{p}_V$ the orthogonal projection on the spine $V$ of $\mathbf{C}_0$, and for $\|T\|$-a.e. $q$ denote by $\mathbf{p}_{\vec{T} (q)^\perp}$ the projection on the orthogonal complement of the tangent plane to $T$ at $q$. Then \begin{gather}\label{e.reverse poincare} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{\sfrac{11}{6}}}\left(\left|\mathbf{p}_V\cdot \mathbf{p}_{\vec{T} (q)^\perp}\right|^2 \, + \frac{|q^\perp|^2}{\lvertq\rvert^{m+2}}\right) \,d\|T\| (q) \leq C \, (\mathbf{E}+\mathbf{A}^2)\, , \end{gather} where $|\cdot|$ is the Hilbert-Schmidt norm and the constant $C$ depends upon $(m,n,p, \mathbf{S}_0,\beta)$. \end{proposition}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{lemma}\label{l:shifting-points} Under the assumptions of Theorem \ref{t:Hardt-Simon-main} the following holds provided $\varepsilon_2$ and $\eta_3$ are chosen sufficiently small. Let $\lambda \in \left(\sfrac{1}{2R_0},\frac{1}{R_0}\right)$, $q_0$ be as in Theorem \ref{t:Hardt-Simon-main} and $\tilde{\mathbf{C}}$ be the cone in Definition \ref{d:new-cone}. Let $O$ be an orthogonal linear transformation of $\mathbb R^{m+n}$ mapping $T_0 \Sigma$ onto $T_{q_0} \Sigma$ so that $|O-{\rm Id}|$ is minimal. Then the assumptions of Proposition \ref{prop:density-est} %\footnote{i.e the assumptions of the graphical parametrization theorem \ref{thm:graph_v1} with $\varepsilon_1$ determined by the $\beta_1$ of Proposition \ref{prop:density-est}} hold when we replace $T$ with $T_{q_0,\lambda} = (\eta_{q_0,\lambda})_\sharp T$, $\Sigma$ with $\Sigma_{q_0,\lambda}:= (\Sigma-q_0)/\lambda$, and the cones $\mathbf{C}_0$ and $\mathbf{C}$ with cones $O( \mathbf{C}_0)$ and $O(\tilde{\mathbf{C}})$. % Moreover, after introducing the cone {\color{red}$\tilde{\mathbf{C}}_{q_0,\lambda}$} of Corollary \ref{cor:reparametrization} corresponding to the new choices $T_{q_0,\lambda},\Sigma_{q_0,\lambda}, O(\mathbf{C}_0)$, and $O(\mathbf{C})$, the assumptions of Proposition % \ref{prop:decay-growth-est} and Proposition \ref{prop:density-est} hold with $T_{q_0,\lambda}$ and $\tilde{\mathbf{C}}$ replacing $T$ and $\mathbf{C}$. \end{lemma}
definitions: Let $\lambda \in \left(\sfrac{1}{2R_0},\frac{1}{R_0}\right)$, $q_0$ be as in Theorem \ref{t:Hardt-Simon-main} and $\tilde{\mathbf{C}}$ be the cone in Definition \ref{d:new-cone}.
Let $O$ be an orthogonal linear transformation of $\mathbb R^{m+n}$ mapping $T_0 \Sigma$ onto $T_{q_0} \Sigma$ so that $|O-{\rm Id}|$ is minimal.

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{ipotesi}\label{a:leone-simone} We let $T, \Sigma, \mathbf{C}_0, \mathbf{C}, \mathbf{S}_0$, and $\mathbf{S}$ be as in Assumption \ref{a:graphical}. $\beta_1$ is the constant of Theorem \ref{t:Hardt-Simon-main}, which depends only upon $\mathbf{S}_0$. For any fixed $\beta<\beta_1$ we choose $\eta_3$ and $\varepsilon_2$, depending upon $(m,n,p,\mathbf{S}_0, \beta)$, as in Theorem \ref{t:Hardt-Simon-main} and we assume that \begin{align} &\hat\mathscr{F}^p_{{\mathbf{B}}_{R_0}}(T-\mathbf{C}_0) + \hat\mathscr{F}^p_{{\mathbf{B}}_{R_0}}(\mathbf{C}-\mathbf{C}_0) < \eta_3\,,\label{e:hyp_flat_T_C0-aggiornata}\\ &\mathbf{A}+ \mathbf{E}(\mathbf{C}, \mathbf{S}_0, 0, R_0) + \mathbf{E}(T, \mathbf{S}, 0, R_0)\leq \varepsilon_2^2\, . \label{e:hyp_graph_aggiornata} \end{align} \end{ipotesi}

Let $\beta_1$ be as in Assumption \ref{a:leone-simone}.

Furthermore, let $u$, $U_{4\mathcal W}$, $\tilde l$, and $w$ be as in Theorem \ref{thm:graph v2}. semantic_equivalence_artifact.normalized_natural_language_theorem: Quantifiers: exists Assumptions: [No-holes property]\label{prop:no-holes} Let $\mathbf{C}_0$ be as in Assumption \ref{ass:cone}, with \[ \mathbf{S}_0 = \mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}_0) \subset \pi_0 \] and let $V$ be the spine of $\mathbf{C}_0$.; For every $\delta \in \left(0, \frac18 \right)$, there exists $\varepsilon_{{\rm NH}} = \varepsilon_{\rm NH}(m,p,\mathbf{S}_0,\delta) > 0$ with the following property.; Let $\Sigma$ be as in Assumption \ref{assumption:manifolds and currents}, and let $T$ be area minimizing ${\rm mod}(p)$ in $\Sigma \cap \mathbf{B}_2$ with $(\partial T) \mathbin{\vrule height 1.6ex depth 0pt width %measure restriction 0.13ex\vrule height 0.13ex depth 0pt width 1.3ex} \mathbf{B}_1 = 0\, {\rm mod}(p)$.; If $\hat\mathscr{F}^p_{{\mathbf{B}}_1}(T-\mathbf{C}_0) < \eta_1(\mathbf{S}_0)$ (where $\eta_1(\mathbf{S}_0)$ is the parameter defined in Lemma \ref{lem:qualitative flat_excess}), and if \begin{equation} \label{e:no_holes} \mathbf{A}+ \mathbf{E}(T,\mathbf{S}_0,0,1) \leq \varepsilon_{{\rm NH}}^2\,, \end{equation} then $T$ satisfies the following \emph{$\delta$-no-holes condition w.r.t.; $\mathbf{C}_0$ in $B^{m-1}_{\sfrac{1}{2}} \subset V$}: \begin{itemize} \item[(NH)] for any $y \in B_{\sfrac{1}{2}}^{m-1}$, there exists $q \in \mathbf{B}_\delta((0,y))$ such that $\Theta_T(q) \geq \Theta_{\mathbf{C}_0}(0) = \sfrac{p}{2}$. Conclusion: \end{itemize}

semantic_equivalence_artifact.normalized_natural_language_theorem: Assumptions: \label{c:scoppia} Consider a blow-up sequence $(T_k, \mathbf{C}_k)$ as in Definition \ref{d:scoppia}, set $\beta = \frac{\beta_1}{2}$ for $\beta_1$ as in Theorem \ref{thm:est spine}, consider $w^k, U^k, h^k, l^k$, $\xi^k$, and $\varpi^k$ as in ($\alpha$)-($\varepsilon)$, and set $\bar w^k:= \mathbf{E}_k^{-\sfrac{1}{2}} w^k$, $\bar\xi^k:= \mathbf{E}_k^{-\sfrac{1}{2}} \xi^k$, and $\bar\varpi^k := \mathbf{E}_k^{-\sfrac{1}{2}} \varpi^k$.; Up to subsequences, the following holds: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] $h^k (i,j)$ is constant for every $i$ and $j$;; \item[(iv)] The following estimates hold (for a geometric constant $C$ which depends only on $p$, $m$ and $n$): \begin{align} \label{bu:c_onealpha} &\sup_{\zeta = (t,y) \in U^\infty} t^{\frac{m}{2}+1}\left( t^{-1} \lvert#1\rvert{\bar w (\zeta)} + \lvert#1\rvert{D\bar w (\zeta)}+ t^{\sfrac12} [D\bar w]_{\sfrac12}(\zeta) \right)\le C\\ \label{bu:radial} &\sum_{i,j} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{1/2}\cap U^\infty_i} |z|^{2-m}\left|\partial_r \frac{\bar w_{i,j} (z)}{|z|}\right|^2\, dz \leq C\\ \label{bu:nonconcentration w12} &\sum_{i,j}\int_{\mathbf{B}_{1/2}\cap U^\infty_i} \frac{|\bar w_{i,j} - \mathbf{p}_{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}^{\perp_0}} (\bar \xi)|^2}{|x|^{\frac{5}{2}}} +\frac{|\nabla \bar w_{i,j}|^2}{|x|^{\frac{1}{2}}}\, dz \leq C\, . Conclusion: \item[(ii)] $\bar w^k$ converges locally in $C^1$ to a $p$-multifunction $\bar w$ on $U^\infty \cap \{\lvert\zeta\rvert< \sfrac{1}{2}\}$ over $\mathbf{S}_0$ taking values in $\pi_0$; \item[(iii)] $\bar \xi^k$ converges locally uniformly to a binding function $\bar \xi$ defined on $U^\infty \cap \{\lvert\zeta\rvert< \sfrac{1}{2}\}$, whereas $\bar\varpi^k$ converges locally uniformly to zero; \end{align} \end{itemize}

Conclusion: \end{equation} \item[(ii)] The following estimate holds (for, we recall, $\mathbf{S}_k$ the open book $\mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}_k)$) \begin{align} &\limsup_{k\to\infty} \frac{1}{\mathbf{E}_k} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{1/2}} \mathrm{dist}(q, \mathbf{S}_k)^2\, d\|T_k\|\leq \int_{\mathbf{B}_{1/2}\cap U^\infty} |\bar w|^2\, d\|\mathbf{C}_0\|\, . \end{equation} \end{itemize}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{proposition}\label{p:scoppia} Let $T_k,\bar w^k, \bar \xi^k, U^k$, $\bar w, \bar \xi$, and $U^\infty$ be as in Corollary \ref{c:scoppia}. Then: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] The converge of $\bar w_k$ to $\bar w$ is strong in the sense that, for $|x| (q) = \mathrm{dist} (q,V)$, \begin{equation}\label{e:strong-L2} \int_{U^\infty \cap \mathbf{B}_{1/2}} (|\bar w|^2 + |x|^2 |D\bar w|^2)\, d\|\mathbf{C}_0\| = \lim_{k\to\infty} \int_{U^k \cap \mathbf{B}_{1/2}} (|\bar w^k|^2 + |x|^2 |D\bar w^k|^2)\, d\|\mathbf{C}_0\| < \infty\, . \end{equation} \item[(ii)] The following estimate holds (for, we recall, $\mathbf{S}_k$ the open book $\mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}_k)$) \begin{align} &\limsup_{k\to\infty} \frac{1}{\mathbf{E}_k} \int_{\mathbf{B}_{1/2}} \mathrm{dist}(q, \mathbf{S}_k)^2\, d\|T_k\|\leq \int_{\mathbf{B}_{1/2}\cap U^\infty} |\bar w|^2\, d\|\mathbf{C}_0\|\, . \end{align} \item[(iii)] $\bar w_{i,j}$ is (locally) smooth in $U^\infty_i \cap \mathbf{B}_{1/2}$ and $\Delta \bar w_{i,j} =0$, for every $i$ and $j$; \item[(iv)] for any $W\in C^\infty_c (\mathbf{B}_{1/2}, \mathbb R^{m+n})$ cylindrical with respect to $V$ we have \begin{equation}\label{e:Simon-test} \sum_i \int_{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}} \sum_j \nabla \bar w_{i,j} : \nabla \frac{\partial W}{\partial y_l}\, dz = 0\, \qquad\forall\, l=1, \ldots , m-1\, . \end{equation} \end{itemize} \end{proposition}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{proposition}\label{p:decad-lineare} There is a constant $C$ (which depends only on $m$) with the following property. Let $\bar w$ be as in Corollary \ref{c:scoppia}. Then there are a linear map $b: V\to V^{\perp_0}$ and a linear $p$-multifunction $a=\{a_{i,j}\}$ over $\mathbf{S}_0$ (taking also values in $\pi_0)$ such that $\|a\|_{L^\infty (\mathbf{S}_0\cap \mathbf{B}_1)} + \|b\|_{L^\infty (\mathbf{S}_0\cap \mathbf{B}_1)} \leq C$ and the following holds for all $0<\rho<r<\frac{1}{2}$: \begin{equation}\label{e:decad-lineare} \sum_i \int_{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}\cap \mathbf{B}_\rho} \sum_j \big|\bar{w}_{i,j} (x,y) - a_{i,j} (x) - \mathbf{p}_{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}^{\perp_0}} (b (y))\big|^2 \leq C \left(\frac{\rho}{r}\right)^{m+4} \int_{\mathbf{C}_0\cap \mathbf{B}_r} |\bar w|^2 \, . \end{equation} \end{proposition}

theorem l_media
 {m n : ℕ}
 (w : Fin m → Fin n → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
 (omega : ℝ → ℝ)
 (V : Set (ℝ × ℝ))
 (B : Set (ℝ × ℝ))
 (H : Fin (m - 1) → Set (ℝ × ℝ))
 (L : ℝ → ℝ)
 (C0 : ℝ)
 (homega_harmonic : ∀ x ∈ B, IsHarmonicOn omega B)
 (homega_extension : ∀ x ∈ B, IsHarmonicOn (fun y => omega y) B)
 (homega_deriv : ∀ l : Fin (m - 1), ∀ x ∈ V ∩ B, (deriv (deriv omega x)).1 l = 0)
 (homega_zero : omega 0 = 0)
 (homega_perp : ∀ y ∈ V ∩ B, omega y ∈ {z | z ∈ Vo})
 (hL_linear : IsLinearMap ℝ L)
 (hL_depends : ∀ C0, L = L)
 : ∀ i : Fin m, ∀ j : Fin n, ∀ y ∈ V ∩ B, w i j (0, y) = (L (omega y)) i j

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{lemma}\label{l:media} Let $\bar w$ be as in Corollary \ref{c:scoppia} and define $\omega$ as in \eqref{e:media}. Then \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] $\omega$ is harmonic and can be extended to a harmonic function (still denoted $\omega$) on $B_{1/2}\subset \mathbb R^m$ with the property that $\frac{\partial^2 \omega}{\partial t\partial y_l} =0$ on $V\cap B_{1/2}$ for every $l=1, \ldots, m-1$; \item[(ii)] $\omega (0) =0$ and $\omega (0,y)$ takes values in $V^{\perp_0}$; \item[(iii)] There is a linear map $L: \mathbb R^{m+n}\to V^{\perp_0}$, which depends only on $\mathbf{C}_0$, such that each $\bar{w}_{i,j} (0,y)= \mathbf{p}_{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}^{\perp_0}} (L (\omega (0,y)))$ for every $y\in V \cap \mathbf{B}_{1/2}$. In particular, for each $i$ the functions $\{\bar w_{i,j}\}_j$ have the same trace on $V \cap \mathbf{B}_{1/2}$. \end{itemize} \end{lemma}

context_pack.section: An elementary lemma on harmonic functions
context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{lemma}\label{l:elementare-Watson} There is a constant $C= C(m) > 0$ with the following property. Let $B_r \subset \mathbb R^m$ and $\omega\in L^2(B_r, \mathbb R^N)$ be a harmonic function such that $\frac{\partial \omega}{\partial t}$ is constant on $V\cap B_r$. Then \begin{equation}\label{e:controllo-da-sinistra} \int_{B_r} |\omega|^2 \leq C \int_{B_r^+} |\omega|^2\, . \end{equation} \end{lemma}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{corollary}\label{c:decaduto-A<<E} Let $\mathbf{C}_0$ be as in Assumption \ref{ass:cone}, with $\mathbf{S}_0 = \mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}_0) \subset \pi_0$ and let $V$ be the spine of $\mathbf{C}_0$. There are a threshold $\rho^+ \in \left(0,1\right)$ and a constant $\bar C$, depending only on $\mathbf{C}_0$, $m$, $n$, and $p$, such that, if $\rho^-<\rho^+$ is a second positive number, then the following properties hold, provided $\eta_5 = \eta_5 (\rho^-)>0$ is chosen sufficiently small. Assume $T, \Sigma, \mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{S}$ are as in Theorem \ref{thm:est spine} with $\eta_5$ replacing $\eta_3$ and $\varepsilon_2$ in \eqref{e:hyp_flat_T_C0-aggiornata}-\eqref{e:hyp_graph_aggiornata}. Assume in addition that $\mathbf{A} \leq \eta_5 \mathbf{E}$, and let $\tilde l = \tilde l_{i,j}$ be the linear $p$-multifunction of Theorem \ref{thm:est spine}. Then there are a rotation $O$ of $\pi_0$ and a linear $p$-multifunction $l^+_{i,j}$ with the following properties: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] $|O-{\rm Id}| + \|l^+\|_{L^\infty (\mathbf{S}_0\cap \mathbf{B}_1)} \leq \bar C \mathbf{E}^{1/2}$; \item[(ii)] If $\mathbf{C}^+$ is the cone realized as $p$-multigraph over $\mathbf{S}_0$ of $\tilde l_{i,j} + l^+_{i,j}$ and $\mathbf{C}' := O_\sharp \mathbf{C}^+$, then \begin{equation}\label{e:decaduto} \rho^{-m-2} \int_{\mathbf{B}_\rho} \mathrm{dist}(q, \mathrm{spt}(\mathbf{C}'))^2 d\|T\| (q) \leq \frac{\rho}{R_0} \mathbf{E}\qquad \forall \rho\in [\rho^-, \rho^+]\, . \end{equation} \end{itemize} \end{corollary}

entries.lean_statement: theorem c_struttura (Σ T Ω : Type*) (q : ℝ) (m p N0 : ℕ) (V : Type*) (U : Type*) (C0 : Type*) (H0 : Fin N0 → Type*) (κ0 : Fin N0 → ℕ) (h1 : dim Σ = dim T + 1) (h2 : dim Σ = m + 1) (h3 : q ∈ spt T) (h4 : ∃ (C0 : Type*), C0 = ∑ i : Fin N0, κ0 i • H0 i) (h5 : ∀ i : Fin N0, H0 i ∈ pages (spt C0)) (h6 : ∀ i : Fin N0, κ0 i ∈ Set.Icc 1 (p / 2)) (h7 : ∑ i : Fin N0, κ0 i = p) : ∃ (U : ℝ), IsOpen U ∧ q ∈ U ∧ IsClassicalFreeBoundary (Sing T ∩ U) ∧ (∀ i : Fin N0, coeff (Sing T ∩ U) i = κ0 i) ∧ (Tangent (Sing T ∩ U) q = V) ∧ (∀ i : Fin N0, Tangent (Γ i ∩ U) q = H0 i)

semantic_equivalence_artifact.normalized_natural_language_theorem: Assumptions: \label{c:struttura} Consider $\Sigma, T$, and $\Omega$ as in Definition \ref{def:am_modp} and assume that $\dim (\Sigma) = \dim (T) +1 = m+1$.; Assume $q\in \mathrm{spt} (T)$ is a point where a tangent cone $\mathbf{C}_0$ has $(m-1)$-dimensional spine $V$, i.e.; it takes the form \[ \mathbf{C}_0 = \sum_{i=1}^{N_0} \kappa_{0,i} \left\llbracket{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}}\right\rrbracket\, , \] where $\mathbf{H}_{0,i}$ are the (distinct) pages of the open book $\mathbf{S}_0 = \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}_0)$ and $\kappa_{0,i}\in \mathbb N \cap \left[1,\frac{p}{2}\right)$ are such that $\sum_i \kappa_{0,i} = p$. Conclusion: Then there is a neighborhood $U$ of $q$ such that $\mathrm{Sing} (T)\cap U$ is a classical free boundary as in Definition \ref{def:free-boundary}, with the additional information that: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] The coefficients $k_i$ in Definition \ref{def:free-boundary} coincide with $\kappa_{0,i}$; \item[(ii)] The tangent to $\mathrm{Sing} (T)$ at $q$ is $V$; \item[(iii)] The tangent to each $\Gamma_i$ at $q$ is $\mathbf{H}_{0,i}$. \end{itemize}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{corollary}\label{c:struttura} Consider $\Sigma, T$, and $\Omega$ as in Definition \ref{def:am_modp} and assume that $\dim (\Sigma) = \dim (T) +1 = m+1$. Assume $q\in \mathrm{spt} (T)$ is a point where a tangent cone $\mathbf{C}_0$ has $(m-1)$-dimensional spine $V$, i.e. it takes the form \[ \mathbf{C}_0 = \sum_{i=1}^{N_0} \kappa_{0,i} \left\llbracket{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}}\right\rrbracket\, , \] where $\mathbf{H}_{0,i}$ are the (distinct) pages of the open book $\mathbf{S}_0 = \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}_0)$ and $\kappa_{0,i}\in \mathbb N \cap \left[1,\frac{p}{2}\right)$ are such that $\sum_i \kappa_{0,i} = p$. Then there is a neighborhood $U$ of $q$ such that $\mathrm{Sing} (T)\cap U$ is a classical free boundary as in Definition \ref{def:free-boundary}, with the additional information that: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] The coefficients $k_i$ in Definition \ref{def:free-boundary} coincide with $\kappa_{0,i}$; \item[(ii)] The tangent to $\mathrm{Sing} (T)$ at $q$ is $V$; \item[(iii)] The tangent to each $\Gamma_i$ at $q$ is $\mathbf{H}_{0,i}$. \end{itemize} \end{corollary}

assumptions: \label{c:struttura} Consider $\Sigma, T$, and $\Omega$ as in Definition \ref{def:am_modp} and assume that $\dim (\Sigma) = \dim (T) +1 = m+1$.
Assume $q\in \mathrm{spt} (T)$ is a point where a tangent cone $\mathbf{C}_0$ has $(m-1)$-dimensional spine $V$, i.e.

it takes the form \[ \mathbf{C}_0 = \sum_{i=1}^{N_0} \kappa_{0,i} \left\llbracket{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}}\right\rrbracket\, , \] where $\mathbf{H}_{0,i}$ are the (distinct) pages of the open book $\mathbf{S}_0 = \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}_0)$ and $\kappa_{0,i}\in \mathbb N \cap \left[1,\frac{p}{2}\right)$ are such that $\sum_i \kappa_{0,i} = p$.
translation_statement_schema.claim: Then there is a neighborhood $U$ of $q$ such that $\mathrm{Sing} (T)\cap U$ is a classical free boundary as in Definition \ref{def:free-boundary}, with the additional information that: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] The coefficients $k_i$ in Definition \ref{def:free-boundary} coincide with $\kappa_{0,i}$; \item[(ii)] The tangent to $\mathrm{Sing} (T)$ at $q$ is $V$; \item[(iii)] The tangent to each $\Gamma_i$ at $q$ is $\mathbf{H}_{0,i}$. \end{itemize}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{corollary}[$\varepsilon$-regularity] \label{eps_reg} Let $p\in \mathcal{N} \setminus \{0,1,2\}$ and $\mathbf{C}_0$ be as in Assumption \ref{ass:cone}, with $(m-1)$-dimensional spine $V$, i.e. of the form \[ \mathbf{C}_0 = \sum_{i=1}^{N_0} \kappa_{0,i} \left\llbracket{\mathbf{H}_{0,i}}\right\rrbracket\, , \] where $\mathbf{H}_{0,i}$ are the (distinct) pages of the open book $\mathbf{S}_0 = \mathrm{spt} (\mathbf{C}_0)$ and $\kappa_{0,i}\in \mathbb N \cap \left[1,\frac{p}{2}\right)$ are such that $\sum_i \kappa_{0,i} = p$. Then there is a constant $\bar\eta>0$ depending only on $p,m,n$ and $\mathbf{C}_0$ with the following property. If $T,\Sigma, \Omega$ and $q$ are as in Assumption \ref{ass:cone} with $\eta=\bar{\eta}$, then $\mathrm{Sing} (T)\cap B_{\sfrac1{10}}(q)$ is a classical free boundary as in Definition \ref{def:free-boundary}, with the additional information that: \begin{itemize} \item[(i)] The coefficients $k_i$ in Definition \ref{def:free-boundary} coincide with $\kappa_{0,i}$; \item[(ii)] The tangent to $\mathrm{Sing} (T)$ at $q$ is $V$; \item[(iii)] The tangent to each $\Gamma_i$ at $q$ is $\mathbf{H}_{0,i}$. \end{itemize} \end{corollary}

context_pack.section: On two notions of flat distance
context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{proposition}\label{flat:same topology} Let $C \subset \Omega$ be a Lipschitz neighborhood retract of $\mathbb{R}^{m+n}$, and let $T$ be in $\mathscr{F}_m(C)$. \begin{itemize} \item[(a)] Let $K \subset C$ be a compact set. Then \begin{equation}\label{f2s} \hat\mathscr{F}^p_W(T) \leq \mathscr{F}^p_K(T) \end{equation} for all open sets $W \subset\joinrel\subset \Omega$. \vspace{0.2cm} \item[(b)] Let $W \subset\joinrel\subset \Omega$ be an open set. For every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists an open set $U_{\varepsilon} \subset C \cap W$ with $\mathrm{dist}(\overline{U_\varepsilon}, \mathbb{R}^{m+n} \setminus W) < \varepsilon$ such that \begin{equation}\label{s2f} \mathscr{F}^p_{\overline{U_\varepsilon}}(T \mathbin{\vrule height 1.6ex depth 0pt width %measure restriction 0.13ex\vrule height 0.13ex depth 0pt width 1.3ex}U_\varepsilon) \leq C_\varepsilon\, \hat\mathscr{F}^p_W(T)\,, \end{equation} where $C_\varepsilon \to \infty$ as $\varepsilon \to 0^+$. % If there exists an open set $W \subset\joinrel\subset \Omega$ such that $\hat\mathscr{F}^p_W(T_h-T) \to 0$ as $h \to \infty$, then for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exist an open set $U_{\varepsilon} \subset C \cap W$ with $\mathrm{dist}(\overline{U_\varepsilon}, \mathbb{R}^{m+n} \setminus W) < \varepsilon$ and a subsequence $h(\ell)$ such that $\mathscr{F}^p_{\overline{U_\varepsilon}}((T_{h(\ell)} - T) \mathbin{\vrule height 1.6ex depth 0pt width %measure restriction 0.13ex\vrule height 0.13ex depth 0pt width 1.3ex} U_\varepsilon) \to 0$ as $\ell \to \infty$. \end{itemize} \end{proposition}
